info.title: Swagger Petstore — Devdocify playground (GET subset)
info.description:
OpenAPI description for the three **unauthenticated GET** operations exposed in the Devdocify Petstore API playground.

**Scope:** This file documents only `GET /pet/findByStatus`, `GET /user/login`, and `GET /user/logout`. It is the canonical machine-readable contract for the curated demo. The public sample server at `petstore3.swagger.io` implements many additional operations (POST, PUT, DELETE, and other GETs); those are **not** listed here.

**Audience:** Technical writers, client developers, and tooling. Use it to generate snippets, mocks, or contract tests for the demo surface only.

**Encoding:** Requests and responses use UTF-8. Successful JSON responses use `application/json` unless noted.

tags.name: Pets
tags.description: Read-only pet catalog queries included in the playground.

tags.name: User session
tags.description: Sample user session flow (login and logout) using GET for demo purposes. Do not treat this as a production authentication pattern.

get.description:
Returns an array of **Pet** resources whose `status` field matches the required query parameter.

Use this operation to retrieve a slice of the store catalog (for example all pets currently `available`). The demo server returns synthetic data; IDs and fields may vary between calls.

**Security:** This GET is unauthenticated on the public sample server.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Inventory state to filter by. Must be one of the allowed enum values.

200.description: Success. Response body is a JSON array of pets (may be empty if no pets match).

400.description: Bad request. Typically returned when `status` is missing or not a valid enum value.

500.description: Server error. Retry with backoff; do not assume idempotency for repeated reads if the server is unhealthy.

get.summary: Log in with username and password (sample)
get.description:
Returns a **plain-text session token** in the response body for the supplied credentials.

**Warning:** Credentials are passed as **query parameters**, which appear in logs and browser history. This matches the upstream Swagger Petstore sample for teaching only—**do not** copy this pattern for production APIs.

**Response headers:** On success, the sample server may include rate-limit and session expiry hints (see response `headers` below).

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: User name registered with the sample server.

200.description: Login accepted. Body is a string (often prefixed with `logged in user session:` followed by a token).

X-Rate-Limit.description: Maximum number of calls per hour allowed for this client (sample server).

X-Expires-After.description: UTC date/time after which the session may be treated as expired (format depends on server).

get.description:
Ends the current session on the sample server. The public demo typically returns a short confirmation message.

**Note:** Session behavior depends on server-side state; for the playground, this call is safe to run without prior login for demonstration.

200.description: Logout acknowledged. Body format is a string on the sample server.

Pet.description: Pet resource as returned by the sample store.

photoUrls.description: URLs of photos associated with the pet.

ApiError.description: Typical error envelope returned by the sample server for failed requests (shape may vary slightly by endpoint).

message.description: Human-readable explanation safe to show to integrators (no stack traces in normal operation).
